questions.question: In each of the questions below, a sentence is given where one part is in brackets. Select the option that provides the correct replacement for the bracketed part. In case no improvement is needed, choose Option D. The enemy (have cross) the Braavos, but they are treading the soil on which they are to be conquered.
questions.explanation: The past participle form of the verb 'crossed' should be used with the present perfect tense construction 'have + verb'. Since the pronoun 'they' has been used to refer to the subject 'enemy', so the plural 'have' will be used in place of the singular 'has'. C is the right answer.

questions.question: In each of the questions below, a sentence is given with one part bracketed. Select the option that provides the correct replacement for the bracketed part. In case of no improvement needed, choose Option D. You will see then that each man (is supplied) with cooked rations for three days and hold the camp in readiness to march.
questions.explanation: The singular verb 'is' as 'each' refers to one. Also, the participial adjective 'supplied' should be used as it is being used to modify the noun 'man'. The part in brackets is correct. D is the right answer.

questions.question: In each of the questions below, four sentences are given, which when properly arranged, make up a paragraph. Rearrange the given sentences and choose the option that provides the correct final order. P. She journeyed on till she came to a house where an old witch woman lived. Q. This witch woman wanted a servant-maid and promised good wages. R. She had to sweep the floor, keep the house clean and tidy, the fire bright and cheery. S. Therefore, the girl agreed to stop with her and try how she liked service.
questions.explanation: P begins the passage by telling us that she came to a house where a witch woman lived. Q follows by referring to this witch woman and telling us who she wanted. S continues by building on Q and telling us that this was the reason why the girl took the job. R concludes by telling us the tasks she had to do as part of the job. PQSR is the final order.

questions.question: According to the passage, which of the following statements is NOT true?
options: Extraverts prefer social gatherings to spending time alone
It's impossible for extraverts to spend time alone
questions.explanation: According to the passage, extraverts find less reward in time spent alone. It is not mentioned that spending time alone is impossible for them.

questions.question: According to the passage, Extraverts enjoy all of the following activities EXCEPT:
options: Finding less reward in time spending alone
questions.explanation: The question asks for activities enjoyed by an extravert person. "Finding less reward" is not a source of enjoyment as mentioned in second last line of the passage.

questions.question: When are extraverts more vulnerable to get bored?
options: When they are in social gathering
questions.explanation: The last sentence of the passage mentions- "they are more prone to boredom when they are by themselves". "They are by themselves" means "they are alone"

questions.question: What is the main theme of the passage?
options: How Extraversion can be a blessing in disguise
questions.explanation: The passage specifically puts forward the characteristics of Extraverts and nothing else. Other options are not described in the passage.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of an extravert?
questions.explanation: Loquacious means talkative, vivacious means enthusiastic and sociable means gregarious. All these are mentioned in the passage. Saturnine means gloomy or sad and it is not a characteristic of an extravert.

questions.question: Four words are given in each question, out of which only one word is correctly spelt. Find the correctly spelt word.
questions.explanation: The correctly spelt word is Fascism

questions.question: Sentences given with blanks to be filled in with an appropriate word(s). Four alternatives are suggested for each question. Choose the correct alternative out of the four. The CEO started his speech with a/an interesting _________ from his favourite author.
questions.explanation: The words 'cliché' and 'platitude' refer to a remark or statement that has lost value due to overuse. Here, they don't fit in due to the use of the word 'interesting'. The word 'crux' means the decisive or the most important point at issue. The most appropriate word is 'aphorism' refers to a succinct observation or saying that contains a general truth.

questions.question: Sentences are given with a blank to be filled with an appropriate word(s). Four alternatives are given for each sentence. Choose the correct alternative out of the four options: The thief _________ with the goods in broad daylight.
questions.explanation: Solution: Run away means to simply run from a place. Run off means to steal things and run. Ran together will be gramatically and contextually incorrect. Ran off will be the correct fit.

questions.question: Out of the fours alternatives, choose the one which can be substituted for the given words. One who has suffered for a great cause
questions.explanation: fanatic - a person whose strong admiration for something is considered to be extreme devotee - a person who strongly admires a particular person or is extremely interested in a subject patriot - a person who loves his/her country

questions.question: Four words are given in each question, but of which only one word is correctly spelt. Find out the correctly spelt word.
questions.explanation: The correctly spelt word is 'Commentary'

questions.question: Out of the four alternatives, choose the one which can be substituted for the given words/sentences. An indirect reference or a casual mention
questions.explanation: Illusion: A false idea or conception, a fake or deceptive appearance. Example: In movies usually ghosts are presented as an illusion Delusion: A misconception or mistaken belief. Example: Delusions of being persecuted is a common symptom of Paranoia Allusion: An indirect reference or a casual mention. Example: The author's new work is said to have an allusion to his lover. Collusion: Conspiracy; secret cooperation.

questions.question: Choose the word opposite in meaning to the given word. Insult
questions.explanation: Insult means to speak to or treat with disrespect. Hence, the word opposite in meaning is "Praise"

questions.question: Choose the word opposite in meaning to the given word. Cliché
questions.explanation: Cliché means used or heard so often as to be dull. The word opposite in meaning is 'Engrossing" which means "Interesting"

questions.question: Choose the word similar in meaning to the given word. Perfidy
questions.explanation: Perfidy means 'the state of being deceitful and untrustworthy'. Hence the word similar in meaning is 'Treachery' which means betrayal of trust.

questions.question: Choose the word similar in meaning to the given word. Ablution
questions.explanation: Ablution is the act of washing oneself. Hence, Cleansing is similar in meaning.

questions.question: Change the form from Direct to Indirect Speech or vice-versa "Do you want some more?" asked the shopkeeper.
options: The shopkeeper asked me if I had wanted some more.
The shopkeeper asked me if I want some more.
The shopkeeper asked me if I wanted some more.
The shopkeeper asked me if I wanted more.
questions.explanation: The simple present tense (want) will change into the simple past (wanted). C is the right answer.

questions.question: A sentence has been given in Active/Passive Voice. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Passive/Active voice. After driving Sankar to his office she dropped him at his apartment.
options: After being driven to his office, Sankar was dropped at his apartment by her.
After being driven to his office, Sankar is dropped at his apartment by her.
After being drove to his office, Sankar was dropped at his apartment by her.
After driven to his office, Sankar was dropped at his apartment by her.
questions.explanation: The participle 'driven' and 'was dropped' should be used here. A is the right answer.

questions.question: Choose the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the Idiom/Phrase and mark it. stole the thunder
options: To take the credit for something someone else did
questions.explanation: To steal the thunder means to take the credit for something someone else did.

questions.question: Four alternative are given for the bold idiom/phrase. Choose the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the idiom/phrase. To turn over a new leaf
options: To shift attention to new problems after having studied the old ones thoroughly
To cover up one's faults by wearing new marks
questions.explanation: The idiom means to start a new life again.

questions.question: In each of the questions below, four sentences are given, which when properly arranged, make up a paragraph. Rearrange the given sentences, and choose the option that provides the correct final order. P. They soon got used to being without Father, though they did not forget him; and they got used to not going to school, and to seeing very little of Mother. Q. She used to come down at tea-time and read aloud the stories she had written. R. Mother was now almost all day shut up in her upstairs room writing, writing, writing. S. They were lovely stories.
questions.explanation: The passage begins with P as it introduces the characters which are to be discussed in the passage. It ends with briefly mentioning "mother". R follows by telling us more about her, and how she used to write. Q follows by telling us that she would at times read what she had written. S tells us how her work was. The final order is PRQS.

questions.question: A sentence or a part of the sentence is underlined. Below are given alternatives to the underlined part which may improve the sentence. Choose the correct alternative. In case no improvement is needed, choose "No Improvement". The courses provided by training institute were more than enough.
questions.explanation: The given sentence is correct and needs no improvement. 'Then' is used to indicate something following something else in time, as in step-by-step instructions, or planning a schedule (we'll go there then there). 'Than' is used for comparisons.

questions.question: A sentence/a part of the sentence is underlined. Four alternatives are given for the underlined part which will improve the sentence. Choose the correct alternative and click the button corresponding to it. In case no improvement is needed, click the button corresponding to "No improvement". Please fill in this application form while you wait.
questions.explanation: The correct phrasal verb to be used here is 'fill out' which means to complete an application or form. 'Fill by' is ungrammatical. 'Fill in' means to take the place of something or someone. 'Fill up' means to fill a hollow object with something else. B is the right answer.
